title: citrus heterophylla
intro: Citrus heterophylla, also known as the Makrut or Thai Lime, is a species of evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia and is characterized by its distinctive, highly aromatic leaves and small, yellowish-green fruit. Traditionally, the leaves of Citrus heterophylla have been used in folk medicine for their antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health issues such as skin irritations and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Citrus heterophylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in traditional herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, Citrus heterophylla is often used in teas, infusions, and essential oils, where its fragrance and medicinal properties can be fully utilized to promote overall well-being.
